sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 57:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 57:1
items.html: The world of Beria is called the world of Kisse [lit. throne], from the word Kisui [cover] and concealment. This is because the light of Hochma is concealed there (see here Chapter 3, item 2). It also implies that the lights of GAR that are imparted there from the world of Atzilut are only regarded as Bina and shine there only in a “sitting” state. It is as one who sits on a chair; his level is diminished. The illumination of Hochma is called “standing”, because in standing the level is complete. (Part 3, Chapter 4, item 4)
